paper_title: Study on Efficacy of Diatomaceous Earth to Ameliorate Toxic Effects of Aflatoxin on Internal Organ Weights in Broiler Chicken

methods.method_name: Broiler Chick Rearing and Experimental Setup
methods.description: This method describes the process of procuring, rearing, and maintaining broiler chicks under controlled conditions for the experiment. It includes vaccination, feeding, and grouping of chicks to study the effects of aflatoxin and diatomaceous earth.

steps.description: Two hundred and forty unsexed day-old healthy broiler chicks were procured from a reputed commercial hatchery.
steps.searchable_summary: procure 240 day-old broiler chicks from hatchery

steps.description: Chicks were reared in battery cage system in experimental sheds with average temperature ranging from 27 to 31 o C and relative humidity of 59% to 62% with 16:8±I h Light :Dark cycle of intensity of 10 to 20 lux.
steps.searchable_summary: rear chicks in controlled environment with specified temperature, humidity, and light cycle

steps.description: All chicks were vaccinated on days 7 and 11 of age with the Newcastle disease virus (Lasota strain) and Infectious bursal disease (intermediate strain) respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: vaccinate chicks with Newcastle disease and Infectious bursal disease vaccines

steps.description: Toxin free and DAE free starter and finisher broiler feed was procured from Department of Poultry Science, Veterinary College, Bangalore, India.
steps.searchable_summary: procure toxin-free broiler feed from Veterinary College

steps.description: The both starter and finisher feed was formulated as recommended by the National Research Council (NRC, 1994).
steps.searchable_summary: formulate broiler feed according to NRC guidelines

steps.description: The DAE was obtained from AGRIPOWER ® , Australia.

The AF was produced from A. parasiticus MTCC-2796 (Microbial Type Culture Collection and Gene Bank culture) procured from IMTECH, Chandigarh, India via fermentation of rice by the method of Shotwell et al. (1966) with minor modifications.
steps.searchable_summary: produce aflatoxin from A. parasiticus via rice fermentation

steps.description: Required quantities of cultured aflatoxin material were added to make the final concentration of aflatoxin in feed to be 0.5 ppm and 1ppm.
steps.searchable_summary: add aflatoxin to feed to achieve specified concentrations

steps.description: The birds were randomly divided into 6 groups, each comprising of 40 chicks.
The experimental birds were subjected for routine checkup on daily basis for the health and husbandry conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: perform daily health checkups on experimental birds

steps.description: Permission from the Institute Animal Ethical Committee (IAEC) was obtained before the conduct of experiment.
A complete record of the clinical manifestations and daily mortality (if any) was also maintained.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain records of clinical manifestations and mortality

steps.description: A total of six birds randomly selected from each group were weighed, euthanized by cervical dislocation and subjected to detailed post-mortem examination.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh, euthanize, and perform post-mortem on selected birds
